title: Cantinoa rubicunda
taxonomy: Cantinoa rubicunda is a flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cantinoa, which is a group of plants closely related to the genus Hyptis. The family Lamiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants commonly known as mint or deadnettle. The Lamiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including Nepetoideae, which includes Cantinoa rubicunda. Cantinoa rubicunda is native to the Americas, specifically in the Andes mountain range.

intro: Cantinoa rubicunda, also known as Mountain Mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the Lamiaceae family, characterized by its square stems and distinctive pinkish-purple flowers. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate digestive issues and promote overall well-being. Bioactive constituents of Cantinoa rubicunda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, such as menthone and limonene, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Cantinoa rubicunda is commonly prepared as an infusion or tincture, or used as a topical application, often in combination with other herbs to support digestive health and relieve pain.
